title: IT Help Desk Agent
description: ### **Description** Arthur Cox LLP is one of Ireland’s leading law firms. We are an “all\-island” firm with offices in Dublin and Belfast. We also have offices in London, New York, and San Francisco. Our practice encompasses all aspects of corporate and business law. With 600 legal staff and a total headcount of nearly 900, we provide a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks, and financial institutions and established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, we have led the way in the legal field in Ireland. For our clients, we work to deliver the best service and support. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. **Department:** IT **Location:** Dublin (on\-site) **Reports to:** IT Helpdesk Manager **Contract Type:** Permanent Full\-time **Background**: Arthur Cox is at the forefront of technology adoption that drive value within the firm, its client's business, and the wider legal industry. IT operations play a central role, connecting digital capabilities to the firm’s objectives and creating a solid foundation for continued success. We pride ourselves on being customer‑focused and service‑driven. Our Service desk operates as a strategic hub and serves as the single point of contact for our employees thus ensuring the customer experience is seamless, proactive, and personalised. **About the Role:** We are looking to hire a Service desk support Agent to join a close\-knit support team providing 24\*7 end user support in a professional services environment and provide On call support after several months, as well as early and late shifts. The successful candidate will act as the primary IT support contact, resolving technical issues for internal staff via phone, email, or chat including: log, analyse troubleshoot hardware and software issues, escalate complex problems to specialized teams, and maintain IT documentation. This role requires excellent written and verbal communication troubleshooting skills, a strong customer service mindset and a willingness to go the extra mile to solve technical issues.### **Key Responsibilities** * Be the main point of contact for internal staff, maintaining first\-class, customer service focused frontline user support whether they are on site or working remotely. * Follow ITIL\-aligned processes and service desk procedures all requests are resolved in line with agreed service level agreements, ensuring all users are treated efficiently, professionally and appropriately. * Troubleshoot and resolve common hardware, software and connectivity issues including: endpoint hardware (laptops and peripherals), Windows 11, Mac OSx, iOS devices, Office 365 productivity suite and business applications. * Escalate complex issues to Level 2/3 support as appropriate. * Assist users with account management, password resets, and MFA support. * Log and track all user requests, incidents, and problems in the ITSM tool, maintaining detailed ticket notes, resolutions steps, and where relevant write/update knowledge base articles. * Assisting with new user setups and on\-boarding, inventory management, staff training and over routine tasks. ### **Skills Knowledge and Expertise** * Degree in IT, Computer Science, or relevant field, or equivalent work experience. * 2\-3 years’ experience in a help desk, service desk, or similar IT support role. * Good knowledge of personal computers, Windows Operating Systems and Microsoft Office 365\. * Hands\-on experience with remote desktop tools, Active Directory and MDM tools. * Familiarity with IT Service Management (ITSM) software. * Certifications (Desirable): CompTIA A\+, CompTIA Network\+ Microsoft 365 Certified: Fundamentals (MS\-900\) Or equivalent Microsoft qualifications **Skills and Competencies:** **Soft skills:*** Excellent interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills in English to both technical and business audiences (individual or group). * Collaborative mindset and who can work independently or more often as part of a diverse team and group of internal stakeholders. * Proactive, flexible and adaptable disposition with a willingness to work hands\-on, demonstrating attention to detail and the ability to take ownership of individual and team’s work. * Innate curiosity and willingness to learn and evolving technology environment on an ongoing basis: develops knowledge base and skill set beyond that called for by immediate tasks and continually push the ‘knowledge envelope’. * Ability to complete assignments with minimal direction. * Strong attention to detail and a methodical approach. * A continuous improvement mindset, continually critically evaluating results to drive improvements of processes and / or methods and/or underlying technology direction **Additional competencies:** * **Customer Interaction:** Ability to engage with customers directly when necessary to resolve escalated issues. Ensure a high level of customer satisfaction through effective expectation setting, communication and problem\-solving. * **Ownership:** Proactive disposition with a willingness for hands\-on teamwork, demonstrating attention to detail and the ability to take ownership of work. * **Communication:** Excellent verbal and written communication skills with ability to build rapport and elicit problem details from end users and explain technical concepts to non\-technical users. * **Problem\-Solving:** Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities. Ability to think critically and develop effective solutions in a time pressured environment. * **Team Player:** Collaborative mindset with the ability to work well in a team environment. Willingness to share knowledge and support colleagues. * **Customer Focused:** Commitment to providing exceptional customer service and ensuring customer satisfaction. ### **Benefits** We reward, encourage and support our talented people to reach their full potential offering excellent progression opportunities along with a market leading remuneration package and benefits including:* Competitive salary and bonus structure * 25 days annual leave * Immediate pension scheme * Comprehensive health and wellness benefits including full gym and wellness programme * TaxSaver and Bike to Work scheme * Wide\-ranging opportunities for involvement in internal committees ### **About Arthur Cox** Arthur Cox is one of Ireland’s leading law firms and provides a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks and financial institutions, established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, the firm has led the way in the legal field in Ireland, delivering the best service and support for our clients. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. *Arthur Cox LLP is an equal opportunities employer. We value diversity and encourage applications from all sections of society. Please advise if you have any additional requirements, we will happily assist. Any information disclosed by you in this regard will be kept strictly confidential and shared within the firm only as necessary and with your consent.* *Reasonable accommodations provided for the purposes of your interview or which you may require during your employment will not be taken into account in hiring decisions.*

title: IT Systems & Security Engineer
description: **Company Description** Are you ready to be a trusted presence in a dynamic and vital environment? At Securitas, our mission goes beyond securing premises — we protect people, property, and peace of mind. As a IT Systems \& Security Engineer you will be responsible for delivering advanced technical support across all levels (1st, 2nd, and 3rd line), managing and supporting core business systems including ERP and Workforce Management (WFM) platforms, and working closely with the IT Manager on cyber security, network infrastructure, and compliance initiatives. This role offers the chance to work with a prestigious clients **Dublin 15** **Fulltime permanent position** **DOE** Why Join Us? * Be part of a company that lives by its values: **Integrity**, **Vigilance**, and **Helpfulness** * Career development opportunities and industry\-recognised training * A supportive team environment * Competitive salary and benefits package * Make a meaningful impact every day Join Securitas and help us **“See a Different World.”** Apply now and become a key player in delivering safety and peace of mind. **Job Description** We are seeking an experienced IT Systems \& Security Engineer to join our organisation in Dublin, Ireland. In this permanent position, you will be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining secure IT infrastructure whilst ensuring the protection of our systems and data against evolving security threats. The successful candidate will combine technical expertise with a detail\-oriented approach to safeguard our organisation's digital assets and support our business objectives. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide 1st, 2nd, and 3rd line IT support across the organisation * Own and support ERP systems and WFM tools (e.g. Timegate, payroll systems) * Troubleshoot complex technical issues across infrastructure, applications, and networks * Manage and maintain servers, endpoints, and cloud services (Microsoft 365, Azure) * Ensure all incidents and changes are logged and tracked in ServiceNow * title: Incident Management Engineer (Korean/English bilingual), Incident Detection and Response
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Amazon has built a reputation for excellence with a mission to be the earth’s most customer\-centric company, a company that customers from all over the globe will recognize, value, and trust for both our products and our service. Amazon Web Services (AWS) is carrying on that tradition while leading the world in cloud technologies. The AWS Incident Detection and Response team is part of the ASPIRE organisation within AWS Support, and is dedicated to offering eligible AWS Enterprise Support customers proactive engagement and incident management to reduce the potential for failure and to accelerate recovery of critical workloads from disruption. We achieve these objectives by working closely with customers to develop runbooks and response plans customized to the context of each workload onboarded to the service. Onboarded workloads are monitored 24x7 by a team of Incident Management Engineers (IMEs) to detect and engage customers on a call bridge within 5 minutes of a critical alarm. ABOUT YOU Incident Management Engineers have a broad skill set with demonstrated career progression and a proven track record of delivering results. The successful candidate will possess strong analytical acumen, solid technology experience, superb business judgment, strategic account ownership and a propensity to dive deep to solve complex problems. You will also have a passion for creating/providing a world class experience for our customers. The candidate must understand the competitive and industry landscape and must have the leadership presence and communication skills to effectively work with customers at all levels of their organization. You must be a self\-starter and able to execute at both a tactical and strategic level – with a strong attention to detail. This is a global role that requires excellent written and verbal communication skills and a passion and desire for leading the resolution of critical incidents. Your decisions are not only fundamental to helping protect our most critical customers but will help maintain the health of AWS customers worldwide. Finally, you are passionate about technology with a desire to learn more and do more with AWS. ABOUT THE ROLE AWS Support is looking for a leader with a strong background in Incident Management and customer ownership to be there during the moments that matter for our most critical customers. We are looking for an Incident Management Engineer to join our team to provide incident response and account ownership. In this position, you will play a pivotal role in providing communication, emergency response, technical resolver engagement and incident management for our customers. Please note as a follow\-the\-sun organisation, IMEs work the core hours of 8am\-4pm UTC \+1\. Successful applicants will be required to work some weekends (Sunday to Thursday, or Tuesday to Saturday), and public holidays. Key job responsibilities * Every day will bring new and exciting challenges that include elements of: * Drive the resolution of large scale customer impacting incidents as part of a team rotation * Drive critical, complex customer escalations in situations that are sometimes technically challenging in collaboration with Engineering Teams. * Provide critical incident response/management (including leading calls with internal/external participants) for customer’s critical workloads * Contribute to Problem Records for customers * Conduct continuous real\-time proactive monitoring of customer metrics * Prioritize, manage, and own emerging and developing customer issues from start to finish * Monitor and manage communications during high impact events via relevant channels * Collaborate with key stakeholders across AWS to improve the customer experience and develop mechanisms that support operational excellence * Lead projects and teams to drive operational improvements * Create and review documentation; design/influence new standard operating procedures * Identify and troubleshoot recurring platform issues and own projects to drive improvements * Mentor peers in your areas of technical and operational strength * Perform other duties as required by the organization About the team Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent * Business level Korean and English, verbal and written * 3\+ years demonstrable Major Incident / Problem Manager Experience for organizations that run mission critical applications * 5\+ years of experience in Support Engineering, Network Engineering, Solutions Architecture, or similar IT role. * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Science/Technology, Communications Engineering, Business, or a related field (or 6\+ years of relevant work experience) * Professional oral and written communication skills, presenting to an audience containing one or more executive team member(s) **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Bachelor's degree * Industry specific accredited certification(s) such as the AWS Associate level certifications * Knowledge of ITIL/Lean Processes * Ability to work in ambiguous environments * Demonstrated critical thinking and logical problem solving skills * Experience driving collaborative projects from conception to delivery * Familiarity operating or designing distributed architectures with the ability to correlate system behaviors based on known inter\-dependencies * Ability to review complex technical details regarding ongoing issues/events and convey the key details to senior stakeholders to facilitate real\-time decision making Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Intern - Governance, Risk & Compliance

title: Lab Apprentice
description: **Job Title** Lab Apprentice **Requisition** JR000015779 Lab Apprentice (Open) **Location** College Park, Dublin \- IRL006 **Additional Locations** **Job Description Summary** Level 6 Higher Certificate in Science – Laboratory Technician Apprenticeship The apprenticeships were developed by BioPharmaChem Ireland, an IBEC sector, along with a strong industry consortium and Technological University Dublin (TU Dublin) Tallaght Campus as the lead provider. These laboratory apprenticeships are suitable for a diverse laboratory environment, e.g. Biopharmaceutical, Pharmaceutical, Chemical Manufacturing, Food, Medical Devices, Independent Testing and State Laboratory sectors. These Laboratory Apprenticeships is a “earn while you learn” higher certificate program that combine on the job industry training with academic study. This two\-year program leads to a Level 6 Higher Certificate in Science as a Laboratory Technician from TU Dublin. During this apprenticeship you will learn the skills, knowledge and behaviours necessary for a successful career in an FDA and EMA approved laboratory environment. You will also gain experience within Keenova. Year 1 Coinciding with Year 1 learning modules the first year of work experience should safely introduce the apprentice to a GMP Lab environment which would involve carrying out the basic duties of a Laboratory Assistant, such as sample collection, sample logging, the ordering of chemicals and consumables, organising sample shipments, pH checks, physical testing, daily calibration checks, glassware maintenance, waste management, archiving etc. The Apprentice should ensure the laboratory is well\-stocked and resourced and be able to maintain good accurate written records of work completed. Year 2 Coinciding with Year 2 learning modules, the second year of work experience should evolve to include supporting solution preparation, the daily calibration of equipment, raw material testing, basic testing such as pH measurement, titrations, bulk density, description etc. along with some of the above. On completion the apprentice will have a broad education and are able to explain basic scientific contexts relating to quality control. During Year 2, the apprentice would be considered a Trainee Laboratory Technician. **Job Description** **Entry Requirements:** The entry requirements for this course are a minimum of: * A pass or Grade O6 in five leaving certificate subjects, two of which must be maths and a science related subject * 250 CAO points, and/or its equivalent * Candidates with a FETAC Level 5 qualification in a related discipline will be considered, * Company Interview and Assessment * SOLAS Apprentice approval *At Keenova, we believe that no one should be alone in their pursuit of better health. This purpose drives us as we pair quality therapeutics with human support—showing up in big moments and small to help patients find comfort and confidence.* *We bring deep expertise and resolve to every therapeutic area we serve, including immunology, urology, men’s health, orthopedics, critical care, and pediatric endocrinology. We work with patients, care partners, and physicians to help them navigate healthcare system complexity and access therapies that make a meaningful difference.* *We are Keenova—keen to solve, keen to serve. Learn more at* *www.keenova.com* *.*

title: Lab Apprentice
description: **Job Title** Lab Apprentice**Requisition** JR000015779 Lab Apprentice (Open)**Location** College Park, Dublin \- IRL006**Additional Locations** **Job Description Summary** Level 6 Higher Certificate in Science – Laboratory Technician Apprenticeship The apprenticeships were developed by BioPharmaChem Ireland, an IBEC sector, along with a strong industry consortium and Technological University Dublin (TU Dublin) Tallaght Campus as the lead provider. These laboratory apprenticeships are suitable for a diverse laboratory environment, e.g. Biopharmaceutical, Pharmaceutical, Chemical Manufacturing, Food, Medical Devices, Independent Testing and State Laboratory sectors. These Laboratory Apprenticeships is a “earn while you learn” higher certificate program that combine on the job industry training with academic study. This two\-year program leads to a Level 6 Higher Certificate in Science as a Laboratory Technician from TU Dublin. During this apprenticeship you will learn the skills, knowledge and behaviours necessary for a successful career in an FDA and EMA approved laboratory environment. You will also gain experience within Keenova. Year 1 Coinciding with Year 1 learning modules the first year of work experience should safely introduce the apprentice to a GMP Lab environment which would involve carrying out the basic duties of a Laboratory Assistant, such as sample collection, sample logging, the ordering of chemicals and consumables, organising sample shipments, pH checks, physical testing, daily calibration checks, glassware maintenance, waste management, archiving etc. The Apprentice should ensure the laboratory is well\-stocked and resourced and be able to maintain good accurate written records of work completed. Year 2 Coinciding with Year 2 learning modules, the second year of work experience should evolve to include supporting solution preparation, the daily calibration of equipment, raw material testing, basic testing such as pH measurement, titrations, bulk density, description etc. along with some of the above. On completion the apprentice will have a broad education and are able to explain basic scientific contexts relating to quality control. During Year 2, the apprentice would be considered a Trainee Laboratory Technician.**Job Description** **Entry Requirements:** The entry requirements for this course are a minimum of: * A pass or Grade O6 in five leaving certificate subjects, two of which must be maths and a science related subject * 250 CAO points, and/or its equivalent * Candidates with a FETAC Level 5 qualification in a related discipline will be considered, * Company Interview and Assessment * SOLAS Apprentice approval *At Keenova, we believe that no one should be alone in their pursuit of better health. This purpose drives us as we pair quality therapeutics with human support—showing up in big moments and small to help patients find comfort and confidence.* *We bring deep expertise and resolve to every therapeutic area we serve, including immunology, urology, men’s health, orthopedics, critical care, and pediatric endocrinology. We work with patients, care partners, and physicians to help them navigate healthcare system complexity and access therapies that make a meaningful difference.* *We are Keenova—keen to solve, keen to serve. Learn more at* *www.keenova.com**.*

title: Legal Receptionist - Onsite
description: Our people are the reason for our continued success and growth. Together, we’ve nurtured a company culture that we’re really proud of. We believe it’s what sets us apart. We strike a balance between approachable and professional, meticulous and innovative. Above all, our people are committed to service and solutions. We are a dynamic and ambitious team of 70 – made up of partners, associate solicitors, solicitors, trainees and support professionals. We have an excellent opportunity for the right candidate. We are seeking an administrator to manage our reception desk as well as provide support on an administrative level to our Operations Manager. The role is full time, Monday to Friday, 9 am to 5:30 pm with 1 hour for lunch between 1 pm and 2 pm each day. The right candidate will be outgoing, personable, enjoys face to face contact with clients, and is a pro at multitasking. The role consists of approximately a 50:50 split workload. **We will ideally look for experience in some or all of the following** **Reception (50%)** * Managing inbound and outbound calls * Managing all Post, DX, Couriers and Deliveries (scanning post, franking post and dropping to the Post Office and DX on a daily basis \- may involve heavy lifting) * Maintaining calendars for Boardroom bookings, keeping boardrooms clean and ready for use at all times, providing catering to clients when requested, monitoring stock levels and managing IT equipment in boardrooms * Organising video/tele conferences via Microsoft Teams / Zoom * Liaising with Clients **Legal Admin (50%)** * File management including scanning, filing, etc. * Managing archives via Iron Mountain and Kefron * Managing various administrative projects * Open new files, maintain existing files and prepare files for closing * Managing Print and Stationery Areas (including ordering stock when required) * Assisting Operations Manager with ad hoc duties and projects **Skills \& Expertise** * 1 year experience as a receptionist / legal admin in a law firm * Pro\-active thinker and problem solver * A high degree of discretion and the ability to work with sensitive and confidential data * Ability to manage a high volume and fast moving workload * Can work on own initiative as well as being a strong team player * Working knowledge of Keyhouse (desired but not essential, training will be provided) * Strong communication skills and an excellent phone manner * Attention to detail Work Location: In person

title: Linux Kernel Engineer
description: At Apple, we craft experiences that have transformed entire industries. The diversity of our people and ideas fuels innovation in everything we do. Imagine what you could build here. Join our Linux Kernel Engineering team and play a central role in the reliability, performance, and evolution of Apple’s platforms. As a Linux Kernel Engineer, you will own the kernel lifecycle end to end, from CI infrastructure and automated validation to driver enablement, debugging, and production\-quality releases. You will partner closely with platform, infrastructure, and SRE teams to ensure every kernel we ship is stable, observable, and ready for real world workloads at scale. **Description** In this role, you will design, build, and operate CI and validation pipelines that compile, test, and release Linux kernels across multiple hardware platforms and Linux distributions. You will lead triage of kernel regressions, investigate crashes and performance issues, drive fixes to resolution, and qualify new hardware at the kernel layer. You will collaborate with engineers across Apple to port, maintain, and upstream device drivers, shepherd kernel changes into production, and continuously raise the bar on kernel quality, automation, and release discipline. Your work will have direct impact on the stability, performance, and debuggability of systems that power critical Apple services. If you enjoy deep kernel debugging, building robust CI systems, and owning kernel releases from development through production, we would like to hear from you. **Preferred Qualifications** Proven track record of upstream contributions to the Linux kernel project or similar upstreams. Bachelor of Computer Science or equivalent skills and work experience. **Minimum Qualifications** Experience developing or maintaining the Linux kernel or low\-level system software (e.g., kernel modules, device drivers, or core OS components). Proven ability to debug kernel level issues (panics, hangs, performance regressions) using tools such as perf, ftrace, bpftrace, crash, gdb, or similar. Deep understanding of kernel tuning for stability and performance across many subsystem domains. Hands\-on experience developing CI/CD systems integrating operating system components and managing kernel releases. Strong programming skills in C, plus proficiency in at least one of Rust, Go, Python, or Bash. Solid understanding of operating system fundamentals: processes, memory management, filesystems, networking, scheduling, interrupts, and hardware/driver interactions. Experience working in a RHEL based Linux environment, including RPM packaging and the RHEL kernel back port and release process. Strong collaboration and communication skills; comfortable working with cross\-functional teams (SRE, platform, hardware, security).

title: Litigation Counsel, EMEA
description: **Responsibilities** Our Global Litigation Team provides key support for TikTok and the family of related products. We empower innovation by delivering integrated legal guidance that supports our business teams in achieving their boldest ambitions. The Global Litigation team is responsible for managing high\-stakes litigation and disputes across all regions. We are seeking an experienced Litigation Counsel to join our Global Litigation team. This Counsel will report to our Head of Litigation, EMEA and AMA and have primary responsibility for leading litigation matters end\-to\-end, managing active disputes through all stages of litigation with a high degree of independence and judgment. The ideal candidate is a highly capable, business\-minded litigator with strong case management skills, a strategic mindset, sound judgment, and the ability to independently drive matters forward. This person should be comfortable operating in a fast\-paced, evolving environment, partnering across legal and business functions, and developing a strong understanding of technical issues and product functionality. Responsibilities \- General litigation support with a primary focus on Europe \- handle general litigation, pre\-litigation claims, and all related matters for the region \- Manage all aspects of litigation including pre\-action correspondence, e\-discovery, fact\-finding and analysis, witness preparation, drafting, case management conferences, interim applications, and ADR or trial preparation and presentation \- Supervise and partner with outside counsel to ensure that all aspects of the company's legal representation are of the highest quality, including the day\-to\-day running of matters, overall strategy, written work, and oral advocacy \- Regularly leads and delivers legal work product with little supervision, including advice to clients on complex matters, contracts and investigation/litigation responses \- Work and communicate effectively with internal stakeholders and support the company's strategy for litigation and where applicable regulatory matters \- Possesses a deep understanding of the substance and nuances of laws in their subject matter area, skillfully interpreting and applying legal concepts to effectively handle various legal matters \- Independently identifies and evaluates legal risk in business contexts, consults outside counsel and relevant stakeholders where appropriate, and makes well\-informed recommendations to clients, including in ambiguous situations \- Takes on a leadership role in addressing complex legal matters, where warranted, exhibiting a high level of autonomy and responsibility \- Considers both business and legal implications of advice with a well\-attuned understanding of risk \- Keep the wider regional team appraised of regional developments and mentor more junior members of the litigation team **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications: \- Extensive experience of managing litigation in Europe \- Qualified lawyer in one (or more) of the jurisdictions in which TikTok operates \- Team player, strong people skills and ability to engage with all levels of the organization \- Proven track record of working under pressure, strong attention to detail, and managing multiple projects simultaneously **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

title: Locum Registrar in Geriatric Medicine (2 weeks)
description: Overview: **Locum Registrar in Geriatric Medicine for 2 weeks (29th June 2026 until 12th July 2026\)** **BEAUMONT HOSPITAL** **(Incorporating St Joseph’s Hospital, Raheny)** Beaumont Hospital, incorporating St. Joseph’s Hospital Raheny, is a large academic teaching hospital 5km north of Dublin City centre. Beaumont Hospital provides emergency and acute care services across 54 medical specialties to a local community of some 290,000 people. In addition, Beaumont Hospital is a designated cancer centre and the regional treatment centre for ear, nose and throat, and gastroenterology, and the national referral centre for neurosurgery and neurology, renal transplantation, cochlear implantation and mechanical thrombectomy. Beaumont Hospital is the lead Level 4 hospital within the Royal College of Surgeons Hospital Group, which includes Connolly Hospital, Rotunda Hospital, Cavan/Monaghan Hospitals, Louth/Meath Hospitals, and the Royal College of Surgeons. Beaumont Hospital employs approximately 4,000 staff and has a bed complement of 820 beds. Beaumont Hospital is the principal teaching hospital for the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland. Beaumont Hospital also enjoys close links with Dublin City University, especially in the area of nurse training, and with other academic institutions in respect of training and research. St. Joseph's Hospital Raheny, is an acute hospital that provides both medical and surgical in\-patient care, day care, outpatient, outpatient physiotherapy and radiology services. A dedicated Care of the Elderly Rehabilitation Unit and a 100 bedded Raheny Community Nursing Unit have also been developed on the St. Joseph’s campus. **Applications are invited from suitably qualified doctors for the following posts which are available 2 weeks from 29th June 2026 until 12th July 2026** **Registrar\- In Geriatric Medicine** Post Specifications: * Each candidate **must** hold a valid **Irish Medical Council Registration** * English Language Competency: All doctors taking up employment who were not registered with the Irish Medical Council in any of the divisions of the Register of Medical Practitioners prior to **1****st** **January 2015**, or who did not complete the entirety of their under\-graduate medical training in the Republic of Ireland, are required to provide evidence of one of the following: * + Completion of your Medical degree in English from any of the following countries: Australia, Canada, New Zealand, The United States of America or The United Kingdom. A copy of your Degree Certificate is the required evidence **OR** * + English Language Tests Certificate: International English Language Test System Certificate (IELTS – minimum overall band score of 7\.0\) **or** Occupational English Test Certificate (OET – minimum overall grade score of B). IELTS/OET test results must be current and dated within two years of the date submitted to the employer. **Closing date for receipt of applications is 3pm on Wednesday 3rd June 2026\.** Applicants should note that it will not be possible to inform all applicants individually of the outcome of their applications. **Beaumont Hospital is an equal opportunities employer and supports a no smoking policy**

title: MSAT Scientist, Cell Culture
description: Location: Dublin, Ireland Job reference: R\-253186 Date posted: 05/27/2026 Are you ready to turn complex cell culture science into robust, commercial\-scale reality that delivers lifesaving medicines to people with rare and devastating conditions? Do you thrive at the interface of lab experimentation and manufacturing, where your decisions directly shape yield, reliability, and speed to patients? As an MSAT Specialist, Cell Culture, you will be a core driver of process robustness and technical excellence from bench to plant. You will design and execute data\-rich studies, scale up new products, and de\-risk commercial operations through smart experimentation, rigorous analysis, and decisive problem solving. Working closely with manufacturing, quality, and regulatory partners, you will translate insights into action and ensure our processes stay reliable, compliant, and ready for what comes next. **Accountabilities:** * Lead, design and execute lab studies to support the process robustness, optimization and characterization of the manufacturing processes, while assessing continual improvements, new material and technology introductions, feasibility assessments that support yield/process improvement initiatives and cost of goods improvement projects that can be replicated robustly at commercial scale, in line with regulatory and commercial manufacturing requirements, including minimizing best practices. * Support large\-scale manufacturing scale\-up activities and New Product Introductions including leading, designing and executing studies such as satellite studies from large scale engineering and technical batches to increase process knowledge, provide recommendations and problem solving to increase probabilities of technical success, while minimizing technical risks. * Support the design and execution of experiments which support process investigations, and resolution of process deviations, root cause analysis, and CAPAs to support commercial operations, and studies in support of regulatory changes. * Analyse complex datasets, interpret results, and prepare detailed technical reports and presentations to cross\-functional teams. * Generate documentation protocols and lead the execution of plant supporting studies and technical studies on the manufacturing floor at commercial scale. * Apply statistical tools such as Design of Experiments (DOE) using software such as jmp to drive robust experiments and data\-driven technical assessments. * The candidate must possess critical thinking skills, be entrepreneurial with an ability to integrate data and information to solve complex problems and to maintain robust and improved manufacturing processes. Effectively communicate complex ideas to audiences with varying degrees of knowledge. * Maintain knowledge and history of the products and process throughout the entire commercial lifecycle, inclusive of development history. * May be required to present/defend technical and scientific approaches in both written and verbal form, including during regulatory agency inspections or information requests. * Ensure compliance with site EHS policy, cGMP and other business regulations and participate in risk assessments, audits, and incident investigations. * May be required to work periodically out of normal business hours or participate in weekend rota to support ongoing lab studies. **Essential Skills/Experience:** * A degree in Life sciences or engineering subject area with experience in (bio)pharmaceutical manufacturing, development or process engineering: + PhD preferred or M.Sc with \> 3 years of experience * Extensive technical knowledge of cell culture (with excellent aseptic technique) in either research or biopharmaceutical manufacturing roles in Technical, R\&D, or Process Development. * Deep technical understanding of cell culture methodologies, harvest technologies, and associated media/solution preparation and associated systems, and an understanding of fed\-batch cell culture processes. * Experience of the set\-up and operation of equipment sets such as small\-scale bioreactors, associated cell counting and metabolite analyser technologies. * Working knowledge of cGMP/GLP requirements. * Good interpersonal skills coupled with demonstrated ability to effectively work in a matrix organization, and in local group settings. * Ability to develop, build, present and defend technical and scientific approaches in both written and verbal form. * Excellent communication skills, and able to effectively influence across all levels of the organization both internal and external to Alexion. * Excellent organizational skills. * Self\-driven, able to prioritize, and to orchestrate multiple activities at once. * Consistently demonstrate sound business judgment when making decisions. * Ability to deal with ambiguity and changing priorities. * Willingness to explore and adopt novel technologies to enhance process understanding and efficiency. * Strong technical writing skills. * Ability to travel up to 10%. * The duties of this role are generally conducted in a lab environment. As is typical of a lab\-based role, employees must be able, with or without an accommodation to: lift/carry items in accordance with manual handling principles; work comfortably in a controlled environment with and around biological, infectious, and hazardous materials; gown/degown PPE; use a computer; engage in communications via phone, video, and electronic messaging; engage in problem solving and non\-linear thought, analysis, and dialogue; collaborate with others; maintain general availability during standard business hours. * Ability to travel up to 10%. **Desirable Skills/Experience:** * Experience with high\-throughput cell culture systems such as Ambr250 is advantageous. * Experience with statistical analysis and DoE. * Experience with Process Analytical Technology (PAT) tools and implementation is beneficial. * Familiarity with emerging technologies in bioprocessing, such as automation, digital lab solutions, and advanced data analytics, is advantageous. **Why AstraZeneca:**Here, your work is guided by the real experiences of people living with rare and devastating diseases, bringing purpose and urgency to every technical decision. You will collaborate in an environment that blends the agility of biotech with the scale and resources of a global leader—where unexpected teams get in the same room, challenge assumptions, and unleash bold thinking. We invest in cutting\-edge science and empower you with autonomy, tailored development, and peers who value kindness alongside ambition. Your expertise in cell culture will directly strengthen reliable supply and help deliver transformative therapies to those who need them most. **Call to Action:**If you’re ready to own the science, shape robust commercial cell culture processes, and accelerate life\-changing medicines to patients, step forward and make your impact with us today! **Date Posted** 27\-May\-2026**Closing Date** 02\-Jun\-2026Our mission is to build an inclusive and equitable environment. We want people to feel they belong at AstraZeneca and Alexion, starting with our recruitment process. We welcome and consider applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of characteristics. We offer reasonable adjustments/accommodations to help all candidates to perform at their best. If you have a need for any adjustments/accommodations, please complete the section in the application form.

are looking for a Multifunctional Sales Associate to join our team. * **8 hours per week** -------------------- * **Monday to Sunday** -------------------- * **Fix Term** ------------ The Multifunctional Sales Associate is responsible for ensuring the best customer experience, contributing to increasing store sales, and maintaining Mango's image and customer service standards, while also performing cashier duties and supporting stockroom operations. **YOUR MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES:** * Customer Experience Excellence: Provide exceptional service to customers, ensuring their needs are met and they have a positive shopping experience that reflects Mango's brand values. * Sales Contribution: Actively contribute to increasing store sales by engaging with customers, understanding their preferences, and recommending products that meet their needs. * Cashier Duties: Efficiently handle cashier responsibilities, including processing transactions, managing returns and exchanges, and ensuring accurate cash handling and register balancing. * Stockroom Support: Assist in stockroom operations, including receiving, organizing, and replenishing merchandise, ensuring inventory accuracy and timely distribution to the sales floor. * Image and Service Standards Maintenance: Uphold Mango's image and customer service standards, ensuring the store is well\-presented and service delivery is consistent and professional. * Product Knowledge Utilization: Utilize product knowledge to assist customers effectively, highlighting key features and benefits to drive interest and sales. * Team Collaboration: Work collaboratively with the store team to support business objectives, sharing insights and feedback to enhance the overall customer experience. **ABOUT YOU:** The ideal candidate is customer\-oriented, proactive, and responsible, with the ability to multitask across sales, cashier, and stockroom functions. They should possess the skills to engage customers effectively, contribute to sales growth, and maintain high standards of service and presentation. **YOUR BENEFITS:** * As a member of the MANGO team you’ll get a discount on all our lines, so that you’ll always be wearing the latest! * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP): confidential helpline, money concerns, general well\-being and legal advice. * At MANGO, we invest in your personal and professional development. Access a wide range of training courses, personalized mentoring, continuous development programs and internal promotion opportunities that will drive you towards. success. * Think big! MANGO offers you international opportunities in over 120 markets for you to broaden your horizons and grow with us globally. At MANGO, we believe in an inclusive culture where creativity and innovation inspire us to take fashion further, therefore, we are committed to providing equal opportunities for all, valuing each person’s authenticity. **Craft your own story** ========================

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Hollyfort, a private company specialising in public works for Government Bodies, including Healthcare and County Councils, is expanding! We're seeking multiple Maintenance Technicians to join our growing team to work on exciting projects throughout Dublin and Leinster. We’re looking for a practical, hands\-on Maintenance Technician who can handle a wide variety of household repair and improvement jobs. If you’re someone who takes pride in fixing things properly and leaving customers happy, this could be the role for you. **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Visiting homes, commercial properties, and public spaces to carry out maintenance and repairs. * Performing a wide range of tasks, including painting, patch repairs, tiling (preferred but not required), and general upkeep. * Undertaking and resolving maintenance problems to a basic level across all disciplines * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance to ensure properties remain in top condition. * Collaborating with tradespeople, general operatives, and subcontractors to complete projects efficiently. * Keeping accurate records of maintenance checks and documenting all work carried out. * Providing a friendly, reliable service that clients and customers can trust. * Proper and safely use of hand and power tools **What We’re Looking For:** * Versatile practical skills across domestic maintenance and repair work. * Experience with painting and decorating, patch repairs, and general upkeep. * Tiling, carpentry, or minor plumbing skills would be a bonus. * A hands\-on, problem\-solving approach and the ability to handle a variety of tasks. * Reliability, good communication, and pride in delivering quality work. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Valid driving license **Why Join Hollyfort?** * Every day is different – no two projects or jobs are the same. * Excellent opportunity to grow and develop * We value skill, initiative, and the satisfaction of a job well done. * You’ll have the chance to use your versatility to make a real difference for our customers. Benefits: * Company Pension * Company mobile phone * Company events * Bike to Work and Tax Saver Scheme * Employee Assistance Program * Learning and Development * Uniform provided Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Company events * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Experience: * Maintenance Technician: 1 year (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Safe Pass (preferred) * Manual Handling Certificate (preferred) * B Driving Licence (preferred) Work Location: On the road

title: Management Company Services Analyst VIE M/F
description: **Description du poste** ------------------------ **Who we are looking for** Someone who is organised, a good communicator and eager to learn and take on new challenges. A self\-starter who can work on their own as well as part of a team. **Why this role is important to us** The role is within the Management Company Services team. This team is managing and coordinating all activities for Funds where Amundi Ireland is the Management Company. This team is currently responsible for €65bn of assets in 80\+ funds with additional funds being added in 2024\. The team ensure that all regulatory requirements relating to these funds are complied with including oversight of Investment Managers, Distributors and Fund Accountants/Depositories. This is a growth activity for Amundi Ireland and we require this role to meet the needs of the expanding business and increased dealings with all stakeholders. **What you will be responsible for** *List key responsibilities \& job duties* * Supporting all central management company services activities for Irish domiciled funds * Interaction with companies where Amundi Ireland have delegated specific activities i.e. investment management, distribution and fund administration * Involvement in the product development process for new funds that are launched which can be across all actively managed asset classes and ETF business lines * Coordination and review of the reports received by Amundi Ireland from delegates which are provided on a monthly and quarterly basis * Interaction with the Designated Person Functions for the management company for Fund Risk, Regulatory Compliance, Regulatory Compliance, Investments and Capital and Financial Management * Liaison with Administrators and Auditors with regard to audit of the Funds Annual Financial Reports and required inputs. * Support in the preparation and filing of regulatory reports. * Assist in the Service Management of delegated administration service providers, and the resolution of service issues. **What we value for the role** * Organised, analytical and strategic thinker, with strong project management skills * A strong level of numerical skills with attention to detail * Excellent team player, interpersonal and ability to interact effectively with colleagues in other business functions and external organizations * An appetite to learn about the management of governance of Irish domiciled funds * Ability to prioritise and work to deadlines * Self\-starter who can work on their own as well as part of a team **About Amundi** What we do. With close to 5,500 team members, and market experts in 36 countries, Amundi provides retail, institutional and corporate clients with innovative investment strategies and solutions tailored to their needs, targeted outcomes and risk profiles. Amundi manages 2\.021 trillion euros of assets across six main investment hubs. * **Critères de candidature** --------------------------- Date de prise de fonction 26/06/2026 Poste avec management Non Niveau d'étude minimum Bac \+ 3 / L3 Formation / Spécialisation **Education \& Preferred Qualifications** + Minimum degree in area of Finance, Engineering, Business or other relevant area, or similar post graduate qualification or demonstrated relevant work experience + Knowledge of MS Office including Excel, Word and PowerPointLangues English (Fluent) * **Entreprise Amundi** --------------------- Premier gérant d'actifs européen parmi les 10 premiers acteurs mondiaux (1\), Amundi propose à ses 100 millions de clients \- particuliers, institutionnels et entreprises \- une gamme complète de solutions d'épargne et d'investissement en gestion active et passive, en actifs traditionnels ou réels. Cette offre est enrichie de services et d'outils technologiques qui permettent de couvrir toute la chaîne de valeur de l'épargne. Filiale du groupe Crédit Agricole, Amundi est cotée en Bourse et gère aujourd'hui près de 2 300 milliards d'euros d'encours (2\). Ses six plateformes de gestion internationales (3\), sa capacité de recherche financière et extra\-financière, ainsi que son engagement de longue date dans l'investissement responsable en font un acteur de référence dans le paysage de la gestion d'actifs. Les clients d'Amundi bénéficient de l'expertise et des conseils de 5 500 professionnels dans 35 pays. Amundi, un partenaire de confiance qui agit chaque jour dans l'intérêt de ses clients et de la société. (1\) Source : IPE « Top 500 Asset Managers » publié en juin 2025 sur la base des encours sous gestion au 31/12/2024 (2\) Données Amundi au 30/06/2025 (3\) Paris, Londres, Dublin, Milan, Tokyo et San Antonio (via notre partenariat stratégique avec Victory Capital)

title: Manufacturing Technician
description: **Job Details** Job Description: **The Role And Impact** Become an integral part of Intel's manufacturing operations, where innovation meets precision. As a Manufacturing Technician, you will play a pivotal role in crafting high\-quality products that drive the future of technology. Working alongside a dynamic, high\-performing team, you will ensure the seamless operation of production processes, helping Intel maintain its position as an industry leader. This role offers the opportunity to work on cutting\-edge equipment, optimize production efficiencies, and see the tangible impact of your contributions on the world around you. **This role is a shift based role. Candidates must have ability to work on a monthly rotating shift pattern. Exact shift details are shared during interview stage.** **Key Responsibilities** * Operate and monitor production equipment to ensure adherence to manufacturing standards and procedures. * Conduct routine maintenance, diagnostics, and troubleshooting of manufacturing equipment to resolve production challenges effectively. * Perform functions associated with wafer production, including equipment handling, process training, and defect reduction. * Implement adjustments to machinery and equipment settings to optimize production efficiency and output quality. * Collaborate with peers and management to meet production targets and escalate issues promptly when output schedules are at risk. * Maintain production logs and required documentation following Standard Operating Procedures. * Drive quality improvements and cost optimization efforts in partnership with engineering teams. * Conduct quality control evaluations, including inspections of raw materials and finished products, to ensure compliance with established standards. * Utilize technical manuals, schematics, and diagnostic tools to perform tasks with precision. * Support the installation, qualification, maintenance, and training activities for new or reused production equipment. **Qualifications** **Minimum** **Qualifications:** * NFQ Certificate (Level 6\) or Diploma/Ord Bachelors/Higher Certificate (Level 7\) in Mechanical, Mechatronics, Electrical/Electronic, Aircraft Systems, Process Technology, Physics, Instrumentation, Advanced Manufacturing, Industrial Automation, Maths, or Chemical Process, or completion of a Trades Apprenticeship. * 0 \- 4 years of relevant work experience. * Proficiency in automated test equipment and data entry systems. * Demonstrated ability to execute tasks with discipline and attention to detail. * Strong analytical skills and a problem\-solving mindset to address production challenges. **Preferred Qualifications** * Excellent written and verbal communication skills to work effectively within a team and across departments. * Proven ability to work autonomously and in high\-performing team environments. * Strong planning and organizational skills with the capability to follow procedures meticulously. * Enthusiasm for continuous learning and process improvement, paired with a proactive attitude. This is your chance to contribute to a company committed to transforming how the world operates while advancing your career in a fast\-paced, innovative environment. Apply today and join Intel in shaping the future of technology. **Job Type** College Grad **Shift** Shift 1 A (Ireland) Primary Location: Ireland, Leixlip Additional Locations: **Business Group** Intel Foundry strives to make every facet of semiconductor manufacturing state\-of\-the\-art while delighting our customers \-\- from delivering cutting\-edge silicon process and packaging technology leadership for the AI era, enabling our customers to design leadership products, global manufacturing scale and supply chain, through the continuous yield improvements to advanced packaging all the way to final test and assembly. We ensure our foundry customers' products receive our utmost focus in terms of service, technology enablement and capacity commitments. Employees in the Foundry Technology Manufacturing are part of a worldwide factory network that designs, develops, manufactures, and assembly/test packages the compute devices to improve the lives of every person on Earth. **Posting Statement** All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance. Position of Trust N/A **Work Model for this Role** This role will require an on\-site presence. \* Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change.

title: Middle Office Operations Associate - Private Credit
description: **Company Overview** KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world\-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. **Position Summary** KKR is seeking a Middle Office Operations professional for its Global Investment Operations Team based in the Dublin office. This individual will work and collaborate with the global operations team, as well as employees in other functions and businesses at the firm. This role is a unique opportunity to become part of an evolving and strategic function dedicated to ‘best\-in\-class’ operations management practices to support our global business growth. **Responsibilities** * Preparation and review of NAV and regulatory reporting * Supervision of daily cash and position reconciliations * Investigate and resolve discrepancies between internal systems and trustees/custodians * Manage external service providers * Record and maintain all lifecycle transactions, capital activity, and borrowings in internal systems * Deliver timely, accurate support to external clients and internal stakeholders * Undertake ad hoc project work, **Ideal Experience** A successful candidate will have relevant experience in fund accounting, fund administration or coming from an operations background (credit/asset management, private equity/infrastructure fund industry experience is preferred). Strong knowledge of NAV calculation on fund vehicles and/or Fund of Fund experience would be a distinct advantage. **CRITICAL COMPETENCIES FOR SUCCESS** * Clear intellectual curiosity and commercial acumen with the drive and potential to succeed in a fast\-paced work environment * Self\-starter – someone who enjoys “rolling up his/her sleeves and getting things done”; has high energy; a strong work ethic; displays the ability to work independently; be creative; and entrepreneurial * Grasps key concepts quickly and displays potential to develop greater specialist knowledge * Solid interpersonal and relationship\-building skills – can effectively develop trusted relationships with many constituents, build credibility quickly, and partner effectively with others * Strong execution and project management skills – highly organized, very detail oriented, proactive problem\-solver, solutions\-oriented, and a knack for anticipating obstacles and addressing issues early on * Efficient and effective work style in a dynamic environment with the ability to multi\-task and work across a number of different work\-streams, assignments and/or projects. Proven ability to manage heavy workloads while remaining attentive to details, and prioritizing time efficiently when faced with multiple projects * Focuses on delivering exceptional client service, with a sense of urgency and follow through * Displays maturity, outstanding judgment, critical thinking, sensitivity and creativity to changing needs and situations. Acts professionally regarding sensitive subjects and maintains confidentiality while exercising discretion at all times * Team\-player – willing to pitch\-in when needed to help on projects, tasks and initiatives, as and when needed; enjoys working in a collaborative and collegial environment; humble and enthusiastic with the desire to learn * Strong working knowledge of MS Office, particularly advanced Excel and previous experience with Advent Geneva would be preferable. KKR is an equal opportunity employer. Individuals seeking employment are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by applicable law. KKR will provide reasonable accommodations as required by applicable federal, state, and/or local laws. Individuals seeking an accommodation for the application or interview process should email Benefits@kkr.com. Emails sent for unrelated issues, such as following up on an application, will not receive a response. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access https://www.kkr.com/careers because of your disability. You can request reasonable accommodations by sending an email to Benefits@kkr.com. Only emails left for this purpose will be returned. **Massachusetts Applicants:** It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. This notice applies only to applicants and employees who work or will work in Massachusetts, in accordance with applicable state law.

title: Mobile Service Technician - Dublin
description: **What To Expect** Our Mobile Service Technicians are unique in the industry, driving in custom fit cars to customer locations to address their needs, ensuring best in class service. At Tesla, our Mechanics are the backbone of the Service operation, supporting our mission to accelerate the world’s transition to sustainable energy. We’ve created one of the most innovative vehicles ever made, and as a Mobile Service Technician you will help ensure an equally innovative service to our customers. We offer: - A dynamic and fast\-paced environment where inclusion, learning and collaboration are key to success. - The chance to work with innovative technology, advanced tools and software. - Ongoing training and development to help you grow your skills and career. - An opportunity to utilise varying models of our fleet for daily operations and repairs. - A competitive compensation and benefits package including Tesla shares. **What You'll Do** - Service, repair and improve: Working with Tesla specific software and drive out to customers to service their vehicles. This is an independent position - Ensure top quality: Fix it right first time, we trust you to inspect our vehicles diligently before return and pre\-delivery and to work with the highest quality standards. - Collaborate: Working across teams (local and regional) you will be sharing support and best practices to further improve the service experience. - Develop: Setting you up for success our dedicated in\-house training teams will train you on Tesla specific technology and the repair of Electrical Vehicles. - Support end to end mobile service process as needed by the business to be able to perform their core duties, with activities like parts management, scheduling, etc. - Customer Facing: Take ownership of all customer facing interactions. You will be required to perform basic repairs, educate customers on technical information and general usage of vehicle **What You'll Bring** - Technically experienced: You have professional experience performing vehicle service and repairs working within either automotive, aviation, marine or similar industries - Technically qualified: You have relevant certification to perform vehicle repairs - A good communicator: You speak and write English and know how to get your point across clearly and respectfully. - Safe to drive: We require you to hold a full European driver’s license (Class B for cars) - Flexible: You may work in shifts to support your team. You may also be requested to work in a Service Centre if needed Join the mission. Apply today. To be considered for our roles in Ireland it is required that you already have the right to work within EU/ EEA. Applications must be submitted online and contain an English CV if possible.

title: Module Engineering Intern

improving manufacturing equipment. * Supports equipment troubleshooting, maintenance, and qualification activities. * Collaborates with engineering teams to optimize process control methodologies and ensure production targets are met. * As an intern, learns and applies knowledge, builds skills, and explores future career opportunities through handson experience and projects that support Intel business goals in a collaborative environment Open to enthusiastic 2nd\- and 3rd\-year students seeking hands\-on experience, with opportunities for **a minimum duration of 11 months.** communication skills \- both written and verbal. * Ability to work independently and as part of a diverse, high\-performing team. * Willingness to work in a cleanroom environment. * Strong attention to detail and disciplined approach to procedures. **Preferred Experience** * Experience with data analysis tools and software (e.g. JMP, Python, Excel). * Ability to summarize data for technical and non\-technical audiences. **What We Offer** * A dynamic, inclusive, and innovative work environment. * Hands\-on experience with cutting\-edge semiconductor technology. * Mentorship from experienced engineers and opportunities to grow your technical and professional skills. * Exposure to Intel's global manufacturing operations and Virtual Factory model. * The chance to contribute to meaningful title: Module Process Engineer
description: **Job Details** Job Description: Intel Ireland is a leading centre for semiconductor manufacturing and technology development, producing advanced process technologies that enable Intel products used around the world. Our teams work in a highly automated, high\-volume manufacturing environment, focused on safety, innovation, operational excellence, and continuous improvement. **Responsibilities / What You'll Do** As a Module Process / Equipment Engineer at Intel Ireland, you will support the development and high\-volume manufacturing of advanced semiconductor technologies. Working in a fast\-paced fab environment, you will collaborate with cross\-functional teams to deliver safe, stable, and efficient manufacturing processes. **In This Role, You Will** * Support the transfer of new process/automation technologies from Technology Development into High Volume Manufacturing. * Take ownership of module and/or equipment performance, including qualification, monitoring, and continuous improvement. * Help maintain high levels of equipment availability and process performance to meet safety, quality, output, and cost targets. * Support preventative maintenance strategies, troubleshooting activities, and implementation of equipment or process upgrades. * Use data analysis and structured problem\-solving methods to identify issues, improve process capability, and reduce variation. * Partner with engineers across process, equipment, manufacturing, quality, and module teams to solve technical challenges and improve operational performance. * Contribute to defect reduction, yield improvement, and manufacturability enhancements. * Assist in driving improvements in safety, productivity, quality, and cost within your assigned area. * Learn, apply, and help sustain best practices across site technologies while developing technical expertise in semiconductor manufacturing. **Qualifications** To be initially considered for this position, **you should have** : * A relevant Bachelor's, Master's, or equivalent qualification in Engineering or Science discipline, such as Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Electronic engineering, Materials Science, Physics, Chemistry or a similar technical field. * 0 \- 4 years of relevant work experience. * A strong interest in semiconductor manufacturing, process engineering, or equipment engineering. * Analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to use data to identify trends and support decision\-making. * Good communication and teamwork skills, with the ability to work effectively across multiple disciplines. * A proactive approach to learning and continuous improvement in a technical manufacturing environment. **Preferred Qualifications** The following would be an advantage: * Knowledge of semiconductor manufacturing processes or high\-volume manufacturing environments. * Experience through internships, university projects, research, or placements in process engineering, equipment engineering, manufacturing, or related technical areas. * Familiarity with statistical analysis, experimental design, or structured problem\-solving methodologies. * Experience working with data analysis tools, technical reporting, or process/equipment performance monitoring. * Familiarity with data automation tools or custom scripts used to extract, clean, and analyze manufacturing data (Python, SQL, MATLAB, R, VBA, Shell scripting, BASH) * Awareness of safety, quality, and operational excellence principles in an industrial environment. **Job Type** College Grad **Shift** Shift 1 A (Ireland) Primary Location: Ireland, Leixlip Additional Locations: **Business Group** Intel Foundry strives to make every facet of semiconductor manufacturing state\-of\-the\-art while delighting our customers \-\- from delivering cutting\-edge silicon process and packaging technology leadership for the AI era, enabling our customers to design leadership products, global manufacturing scale and supply chain, through the continuous yield improvements to advanced packaging all the way to final test and assembly. We ensure our foundry customers' products receive our utmost focus in terms of service, technology enablement and capacity commitments. Employees in the Foundry Technology Manufacturing are part of a worldwide factory network that designs, develops, manufactures, and assembly/test packages the compute devices to improve the lives of every person on Earth. **Posting Statement** All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance. Position of Trust N/A **Work Model for this Role** This role will be eligible for our hybrid work model which allows employees to split their time between working on\-site at their assigned Intel site and off\-site. \* Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change.

description: **Responsibilities \& Accountabilities:** * Clean in accordance with Hospital Cleaning standards * Be observant for spillages and act promptly to ensure they are properly dealt with. * Use appropriate equipment as supplied by Hospital to carry out daily tasks. * Move furniture as relevant to clean behind and underneath, with assistance from other ward/department staff as required. * Clean all floors and skirtings , internal signage, including notice boards, pictures and frames, walls including splash marks. doors, frames, door handles and component parts internal glass and glazing. * Vacuum each landing and damp dust surrounds, skirting boards, evacuation chairs, pictures, fire fighting equipment, signage including notice boards and frames and to clean the lifts.. ***Please see full job description for a complete list of responsibilities***

title: Options Sales Middle Office Analyst
description: **Overview** Joining the Client team as a Sales Operations Analyst supporting the Options Sales business, you will be responsible for Susquehanna’s options sales desk’s post trade processes to provide a seamless client experience. This role involves working closely with our sales traders, traders, and counterparties. Due to the high volume of trades, we are looking for candidates who have exceptional attention to detail. This role will offer a great exposure to a front office Sales \& Trading environment and will be an excellent opportunity if you have strong communication skills and an interest in financial markets. Being directly exposed to client interactions, a good level of commercial awareness is desired. **In This Role You Will** * Responsible for post trade processes * Execution and allocation of orders through internal/external systems * Act as a first point of contact for any issues related to crossing and settling trades across multiple exchanges, countries and clients * Address each client’s specific post trade requirements and tailor the process to their needs. For example, clients trading on CFD or making sure trades with FTT are accounted for correctly. * Identify opportunities to improve the overall process, automate tools where possible in order to enhance the broader desk’s performance and efficiency. This could include working with the static data team to improve our flex trading process and working with app support and developers to improve the desks tools * Liaise with other teams to develop and work on ad hoc projects aimed at improving the team’s workflow **What We're Looking For** * Minimum of a Bachelors’ degree * Ability to prioritise and complete multiple tasks in a fast paced, dynamic environment * High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. Python a plus * Strong communicator (verbal and written) * Ability to work under pressure and multi\-task * Ambitious and self\-motivated, not afraid to ask questions and use own initiative * Effective problem solving and analytical skills; including effective troubleshooting in a fast\-paced, time\-critical environment * Experience in financial markets is not required although we welcome those with experience * Excellent attention to detail **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. title: Part Time Sales Assistant

Get your application in soon to avoid missing out. **Apply now** and join a winning team at Sports Direct, where passion meets performance.
